description: Swarm Alignment Contract and Approval Gates - add persisted role alignment state, deterministic gate decisions, and user-facing approval checkpoints so Swarm can auto-run routine work without drifting on risky runs.

userStories.title: Define alignment state in the canonical swarm snapshot
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want alignment data stored in the canonical swarm snapshot so that contract and gate state have one source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Add alignment fields to canonical swarm state for the project-scoped role catalog, user-scoped confirmations, run contract, gate, role delta status, pending confirmation, and audit metadata
Initialize empty alignment state for newly created swarms
Do not add compatibility loaders for pre-alignment swarm records
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist role catalog and user confirmation records
userStories.description: As a swarm operator, I want project-level role definitions and user-level confirmations stored separately so that teams share role baselines without sharing approvals.
acceptanceCriteria: Persist project-scoped role catalog entries with `id`, `name`, `purpose`, `perspective`, `default_when`, and `version`
Persist user-scoped confirmation records separately from the role catalog
Record audit metadata when role catalog entries are created or updated
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Classify meaningful role deltas
userStories.description: As a conductor, I want requested roles compared against the confirmed catalog so that only material role changes trigger user review.
acceptanceCriteria: Compare requested run roles against catalog roles and classify each as `unchanged`, `added`, `removed`, or `modified`
Treat only `purpose`, `perspective`, and `default_when` as material changes
Ignore timestamps, formatting-only edits, and other metadata-only noise during delta detection
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Generate run contracts before delegation
userStories.description: As a conductor, I want each swarm run to create a compact run contract before delegation so that alignment happens at the direction level.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a persisted `RunContract` with `goal`, `scope`, `constraints`, `roles`, `mode`, `assumptions`, `risks`, `discussion_reason`, and `created_at`
Generate and store the run contract before any worker delegation starts
Store role references as catalog entries plus per-run overlay deltas when present
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Evaluate deterministic alignment gates
userStories.description: As a user, I want gate decisions to come from explicit deterministic inputs so that Swarm interruptions are predictable and explainable.
acceptanceCriteria: Define gate values `G0`, `G1`, `G2`, and `G3`
Implement deterministic gate precedence in this order: action sensitivity, material role delta, ambiguity or valid options or trade-off strength, confidence, routine versus novel scope
Allow LLM output to inform only the `confidence` input, not the final gate mapping logic
Persist the gate, the evaluated inputs, and a human-readable decision reason for each run
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce discussion admission thresholds
userStories.description: As a conductor, I want discussion mode admitted only for real direction-setting choices so that routine execution does not turn into unnecessary debate.
acceptanceCriteria: Evaluate the three primary discussion signals: multiple valid options, meaningful trade-offs, and direction-changing consequences
Allow discussion mode only when at least two primary discussion signals are true
Treat likely benefit from role-based debate as advisory context rather than a hard requirement
Fall back to normal execution planning when the admission threshold is not met
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run conductor preflight from alignment state
userStories.description: As a system owner, I want the conductor to create the contract and gate before delegation so that worker planning follows approved alignment rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Update conductor flow to generate the run contract, compute role deltas, and evaluate the gate before worker delegation begins
Proceed automatically for `G0` and `G1`, and require user confirmation for `G2` and `G3`
Update conductor prompt or hook strategy so it does not ask the user to approve every task breakdown or worker prompt
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create user-facing alignment summaries
userStories.description: As a user, I want one concise alignment summary for higher-risk runs so that I can confirm direction without reading internal worker prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: Generate a user-facing alignment summary for `G2` and `G3` runs with goal, scope, constraints, selected roles, material role deltas, major assumptions, and proposed next phase
Do not include raw internal worker prompts in the summary unless explicitly requested
Store or expose the summary in a form that both CLI and web consumers can read
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pause and resume runs on confirmation gates
userStories.description: As a user, I want risky runs to pause until I confirm them so that Swarm cannot continue past a direction checkpoint without my approval.
acceptanceCriteria: When the evaluated gate is `G2` or `G3`, record pending confirmation state and pause the run before delegation continues
Clear pending confirmation and resume delegation when run-level confirmation is recorded
Skip confirmation when there is no material role delta and the gate is below `G2`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auto-write approved role changes back to the catalog
userStories.description: As a user, I want approved role changes saved back to the long-lived catalog so that I do not need to re-approve the same updates on future runs.
acceptanceCriteria: When a user approves a modified role for a run, write the approved `purpose`, `perspective`, and `default_when` values back to the long-lived role catalog
Do not rewrite catalog entries for `unchanged` roles
Record the run and user that caused each catalog writeback
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Pause on mid-run gate escalation
userStories.description: As a user, I want Swarm to stop when a later phase becomes riskier than the original contract so that I can re-approve the new direction.
acceptanceCriteria: Re-evaluate alignment gate when later phases introduce new risk inputs
Pause the run before further delegation when the new gate is higher than the current gate
Persist the new gate, the escalation reason, and a fresh pending confirmation requirement before the run can continue
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expose alignment read state in swarm APIs
userStories.description: As a client developer, I want alignment state available in swarm read models so that web and CLI surfaces can explain why a run proceeded or paused.
acceptanceCriteria: Expose run contract, gate, selected roles, role delta status, pending confirmation state, and alignment summary in server-side swarm read models
Provide read paths suitable for both web and CLI consumers
Regenerate the JavaScript SDK after the route schema changes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add alignment confirmation write routes
userStories.description: As a user, I want server-side endpoints for role approval and run confirmation so that the UI and CLI can resume or update alignment state safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a server-side write path for role approval that updates confirmation state and catalog writeback according to the alignment rules
Add a server-side write path for run-level confirmation that clears pending confirmation and resumes the paused run
Regenerate the JavaScript SDK after the route schema changes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Show alignment summaries in CLI flow
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want alignment decisions shown in the terminal so that I can understand non-blocking and blocking gates without reading internal prompts.
acceptanceCriteria: Show a concise non-blocking alignment summary for `G1` runs in the CLI flow
Use the alignment summary instead of raw worker prompts when asking for `G2` or `G3` confirmation in CLI surfaces
Include the proposed next phase or pause reason in the CLI output
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add a web alignment panel
userStories.description: As a web user, I want a dedicated alignment panel so that I can see the current gate, role deltas, and pending approvals at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a web panel that shows current gate, mode, selected roles, role delta summary, run contract summary, and pending confirmation state
Explain why auto-running `G0` or `G1` runs proceeded without blocking
Do not display raw internal worker prompts by default
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add unit coverage for gate and discussion policy
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want deterministic unit coverage for gate and discussion decisions so that future changes do not make Swarm too interruptive or too permissive.
acceptanceCriteria: Add tests for unchanged confirmed roles plus low risk producing `G0` or `G1`
Add tests for material role deltas producing `G2`
Add tests for sensitive or high-risk actions producing `G3`
Add tests for `0/3`, `1/3`, `2/3`, and `3/3` primary discussion signals mapping to the expected execution or discussion mode
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add integration coverage for approval and escalation flows
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want integration coverage for approval writeback and mid-run escalation so that alignment state stays reliable across real swarm transitions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add integration tests for role approval auto-writeback and audit metadata
Add integration tests for mid-run gate escalation pausing the run and requiring fresh confirmation
Add integration tests showing alignment state is readable by server-side consumers after approval and escalation transitions
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
